brand_voice_note: Warm, understated and sustainability-first, letting natural materials do the talking.

concepts.primary_text:
Your feet run hot by 2pm. These don't.

The Tree Runner is spun from breathable eucalyptus fiber, so all-day wear feels like barely wearing anything at all. Machine washable when life happens.
concepts.headline: The everyday shoe that breathes
concepts.why_it_works: Leads with the one thing sneaker shoppers feel every afternoon, then ties it straight to the material, which is your real differentiator.

concepts.primary_text:
Most "sustainable" sneakers still ship in plastic and feel like cardboard.

The Varsity is a mushroom-toned retro runner made from natural materials that actually holds its shape. Comfort you don't have to feel guilty about.
concepts.headline: Retro looks, zero compromise
concepts.why_it_works: Names the credibility gap shoppers quietly suspect about eco brands, then positions this product as the honest answer.

concepts.primary_text:
Couch to 5k season is here.

The Dasher NZ was built for the runs you're actually going to do, with a supportive ride and a wool-blend upper that handles cold mornings. Start line optional.
concepts.headline: Built for the miles ahead
concepts.why_it_works: Anchors the shoe to a specific seasonal moment when people buy running gear, which raises intent without a discount.
